title: cassia senna var. eriocarpa
intro: Cassia senna var. eriocarpa, also known as the sweet cassia or cassia senna, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, commonly used for its medicinal and laxative properties. Its seeds and pods contain anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its laxative effects, and it has been used to treat various health issues such as constipation, hemorrhoids, and irritable bowel syndrome. The health benefits of Cassia senna var. eriocarpa include promoting regular bowel movements, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Cassia senna var. eriocarpa is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or powder, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its laxative and medicinal properties.
